senses.definition: Relating to cannibals or cannibalism; consuming the flesh of humans or beings of the same species.
examples: The explorer described the tribe as having cannibalean rituals.
Some scholars criticized the novel for its cannibalean imagery.

senses.definition: Of or relating to cannibals or cannibalism.
examples: The manuscript contains cannibalic imagery.
Some scholars debated the cannibalic symbolism in the text.

senses.definition: The act or process of cannibalising; the taking of resources from one entity to another within the same organization or system.
examples: The new product line caused cannibalisation of the older models.
Market cannibalisation reduced overall profits.

senses.definition: To take sales, customers, or resources from one product or entity by offering a similar alternative; to engage in cannibalisation.
examples: The new phone model will cannibalise sales of the older version.
Marketing plans risk cannibalising existing demand.

senses.definition: Past tense of cannibalise; taken away sales or resources through cannibalisation.
examples: Sales cannibalised the profits last quarter.
The launch cannibalised existing demand for accessories.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of cannibalise; takes market share from a similar product.
examples: The new model cannibalises the sales of the older version.
Marketing efforts cannibalise demand for competing products.

senses.definition: Having characteristics of cannibalism; cannibalistic.
examples: The novel features cannibalish scenes that divided readers.
Some critics argued the depiction was cannibalish in tone.

senses.definition: Present participle of cannibalise; engaging in cannibalisation (consuming resources).
examples: The new release is cannibalising sales from older devices.
There is concern that price cuts are cannibalising profits.

senses.definition: The practice of eating another human or the flesh of one's own species.
examples: Ancient myths describe ritual cannibalism.
The book discusses the anthropology of cannibalism.

senses.definition: Plural form of cannibalism; multiple instances or theories of cannibalistic practices.
examples: Historical records note several cannibalisms across cultures.
Scholars compare cannibalisms to understand societal stress.

senses.definition: Relating to cannibalism; involving the eating of human flesh.
examples: The film contains several cannibalistic scenes.
Cultural taboos often address cannibalistic acts.

senses.definition: In a cannibalistic manner; in a way that resembles cannibalism.
examples: The market grew cannibalistically, taking shares from rivals.
The company expanded cannibalistically, eroding profits.

senses.definition: The quality or state of being cannibalistic.
examples: The cannibality of the tribe was a subject of debate.
Themes of cannibality appear in the novel.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of cannibalisation; the act of cannibalising.
examples: There was significant cannibalization of the product line.
Cannibalization reduced profits in the short term.

senses.definition: Plural form of cannibalization; multiple instances of cannibalising.
examples: Several cannibalizations occurred as the market shifted.
Different products contributed to cannibalizations.

senses.definition: To take sales, resources, or customers from one product or service by introducing another similar one.
examples: The new model will cannibalize older versions.
They worry the price cut will cannibalize profits.

senses.definition: Past tense of cannibalize; sales or resources were taken from one product by another.
examples: Older models were cannibalized by the latest release.
Profit margins were cannibalized during the launch.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of cannibalize; takes sales or resources from one product by another.
examples: The new device cannibalizes the sales of the old model.
Marketing strategies cannibalize profits.

senses.definition: Present participle of cannibalize; taking away sales or resources from one product by another.
examples: The company is cannibalizing its own product line.
Price cuts are cannibalizing profits.

senses.definition: In a cannibalistic manner; like a cannibal.
examples: The market expanded cannibally, absorbing rivals.
He acted cannibally toward his competitors.

senses.definition: People who eat human flesh; plural of cannibal.
examples: The myths tell of fierce cannibals in remote regions.
The group feared becoming targets of cannibals.

senses.definition: Slang or informal for a small can; a nickname or diminutive form of can.
examples: He opened aie, a cannie of juice.
The kid tucked a cannie into his bag.

senses.definition: More cunny? Actually: comparative of canny; more shrewd or prudent.
examples: This approach is cannier than the previous one.
A cannier investor would wait for clearer signals.

senses.definition: Superlative form of canny; most shrewd or prudent.
examples: She is the canniest investor in the room.
Among the candidates, he was the canniest by far.

senses.definition: A small cup or mug used for drinking.
examples: He filled a tiny cannikin with water.
The child carried a metal cannikin to school.
